timelines.notes: Fetched 2026-05-11. Aggregated across Al Jazeera's 2024 US election coverage with focus on congressional races (per user instruction). Challenge: AJ's congressional-specific coverage is thinner than its presidential coverage; user-provided URLs are primarily general election explainers and liveblogs which include congressional content as part of broader election context. Contributing articles directly congressional: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/10/11/why-are-only-some-us-senate-seats-up-for-grabs-what-to-know-in-500-words (Senate explainer — directly congressional) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/10/20/us-election-the-2024-senate-races-that-could-decide-party-control (Senate races — directly congressional) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2024/11/8/us-election-live-trump-begins-transition-as-dems-say-house-is-within-grasp (House status — directly congressional) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2024/11/9/us-election-live-trump-presidency-takes-shape-as-house-hangs-in-balance (House status — directly congressional). Other contributing AJ articles which covered presidential and congressional races together: https://www.aljazeera.com/features/2024/10/30/a-simple-guide-to-the-us-election-2024 ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/10/29/us-elections-2024-everything-you-need-to-know-in-maps-and-charts ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/10/13/state-of-the-race-five-takeaways-from-the-us-election-this-week ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/11/4/how-will-us-election-day-unfold ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/11/5/polls-open-for-2024-us-election-day-as-kamala-harris-donald-trump-face-off ; https://www.aljazeera.com/us-election-2024/results/ (live results page, not dated article — used for outcome verification). Additional AJ articles drawn from broader coverage to anchor key congressional milestones: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/3/5/super-tuesday-results (Mar 5 Super Tuesday + Sinema retirement coverage) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/11/13/us-republicans-set-to-control-house-and-senate (Nov 13 House call) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/11/13/john-thune-elected-senate-republican-leader ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/1/3/mike-johnson-reelected-house-speaker-as-119th-congress-begins ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/1/6/us-congress-certifies-trump-election-win . Date approximations per rule 4: Oct 13, 20, 29, 30 and Nov 4 entries are pegged to AJ article publication dates; these are AJ's coverage events rather than discrete congressional milestones, included because they document AJ's reporting on the congressional race landscape at each point. Two same-day entries on Nov 13, 2024 (House call + Thune leadership election) preserved as separate distinct events per rule — these occurred independently on the same day. May 14, 2024 entry pegged to the simultaneous WV and MD primaries; both decided their respective Senate general election matchups. Source disagreements: 'Republican congressional trifecta' framing — AJ entries describe the Nov 13 outcome as Republicans retaining trifecta with Senate; some commentators distinguished the Senate (newly flipped to GOP) from the House (held). CNN entry phrases similarly. No disagreement on outcomes captured. Public figures named: Sinema, Thune, Johnson, Trump, Kim, Hogan, Alsobrooks, McCormick, Casey, Moreno, Brown, Tester, Slotkin, Rogers — all public officials/candidates of record. No private individuals named.

Fetched 2026-05-11. Aggregated across CNN's 2024 congressional race coverage (per user instruction). Challenge: most user-provided CNN URLs are live results pages or liveblogs without specific article dates; the dated article anchors are the Oct 4 senate race rankings and the Nov 5-6 liveblogs. Contributing articles: https://www.cnn.com/2024/10/04/politics/senate-race-rankings-october-2024 (key dated article — CNN's October Senate race rankings) ; https://www.cnn.com/politics/live-news/senate-house-governor-election-11-05-24 (election night liveblog) ; https://www.cnn.com/politics/live-news/senate-house-governor-election-11-06-24 (day-after results liveblog) ; https://www.cnn.com/election/2024/results/senate (live results page — not dated article, used for outcome verification) ; https://www.cnn.com/election/2024/results/president (live results — context) ; https://www.cnn.com/election/2024/calendar and calendar-past-events (calendar pages — used to verify primary dates). Additional CNN articles drawn from broader coverage to anchor key congressional milestones: https://www.cnn.com/2024/03/05/politics/kyrsten-sinema-retirement-arizona-senate ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/03/19/politics/bernie-moreno-ohio-senate-primary-win ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/05/14/politics/maryland-senate-primary-alsobrooks-hogan ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/06/04/politics/andy-kim-new-jersey-senate-primary ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/08/06/politics/michigan-senate-primary-slotkin-rogers ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/08/13/politics/connecticut-senate-primary ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/11/13/politics/house-republican-majority-called ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/11/13/politics/thune-senate-republican-leader-elected ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/01/03/politics/mike-johnson-speaker-vote ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/01/03/politics/119th-congress-sworn-in ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/01/06/politics/congress-certifies-trump-election-win . Date approximations per rule 4: Multiple same-day entries (two on Nov 5, two on Nov 6, two on Nov 13, two on Jan 3) preserved as distinct events per rule — they cover materially different developments occurring on the same day. Nov 5 Senate flips entry framed as Tuesday evening since CNN's election-night coverage timestamped the WV and OH calls in the early evening hours. Nov 6 'declared Senate majority' pegged to morning-after framing per CNN's day-two coverage. Nov 13 'clinched House control' pegged to AP and CNN's joint call after Thomas Kean Jr.'s NJ-7 win; Thune's leadership election occurred Wednesday morning Nov 13, the same day. Connecticut Senate primary (Aug 13) included as a representative state primary that day; Wisconsin and other states also primaried Aug 13. Source disagreements: McCormick-Casey Pennsylvania race had a recount; CNN's Nov 6 entry uses 'narrowly defeated' framing — the call was made by major outlets on Nov 7-8 after the recount but the night-of margin was visible on Nov 6. The race was officially decided on Nov 21 by court order but the Nov 6 entry reflects when the contest's general direction became clear per CNN's reporting. Public figures named: Sinema, Moreno, Brown (implicit), Hogan, Alsobrooks, Kim, Slotkin, Rogers, McCormick, Casey, Thune, Johnson, Trump — all public officials/candidates of record. No private individuals named.
